response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and communication abilities, they lack the core technical requirements for this role. With only six months of documented C++ experience from December 2014 to June 2015, they fall far short of the required 5-10 years. Critically absent are SQL Server/Oracle expertise, Windows platform experience, and any capital markets or financial services domain knowledge. Their career has consistently moved toward modern web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), making them poorly positioned for a capital markets C++ role.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to master diverse technologies and contribute across full-stack development
Strong communication and mentoring skills through workshops, talks, and teaching roles
Solid software engineering fundamentals with TDD, CI/CD, and DevOps infrastructure experience
concerns: Minimal C++ experience: only 6 months documented (2014–2015), no recent hands-on work in the language
No demonstrated expertise in SQL Server, Oracle, or enterprise relational database systems
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or financial services domain experience

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (5-10 year requirement vs. 6 months documented)
No financial services or capital markets domain background
Strong engineering fundamentals and communication skills
